cases.prompt: Coordinate the approved account-security feature across API, storage, tests, review, and final verification.
Run a coordinated workflow for this multi-component refactor, keeping context, task handoffs, integration, and verification aligned.
Propose an approach before adding organization-level SSO, including impact, alternatives, rollout, rollback, and validation.
Plan this database migration and ask for one decision before editing because the compatibility and rollback approach are not approved.
Find the security instructions, indexed context, implementation pattern, nearest tests, and commands relevant to the authentication service.
Before changing this unfamiliar package, return the smallest verified repository context packet and unresolved conflicts.
Split this approved migration into atomic tasks with contracts, dependencies, overlap analysis, validation, and a critical path.
Convert the approved API, worker, and schema changes into complete task packets and safe execution batches.
Implement the approved pagination fix, preserve my unrelated working-tree changes, add focused coverage, and report fresh evidence.
Update the repository validator to reject duplicate identifiers and run the relevant tests; the implementation is authorized.
Add a deterministic regression test for the pagination bug at the correct boundary and run the relevant suite.
Add focused contract tests for the serializer schema, including the important rejection boundary without testing private helpers.
Review this branch for correctness, security, data loss, concurrency, compatibility, and missing tests without changing files.
Audit the pull request and return only actionable evidence-backed findings with tight locations and realistic severity.
Verify the installed SDK version's current official API contract and cite the exact source before we choose an implementation.
Resolve whether this framework version supports the proposed migration using local version evidence and authoritative documentation.
Before declaring this change complete, map every acceptance criterion to fresh tests, validation, packaging, and residual risk.
Inspect the final diff and verify schemas, generated files, package contents, commands, and material untested paths.
This repository has no canonical Codex context yet. Analyze it and preview the first evidence-backed guidance, indexed catalog, and native agent profiles without writing.
Create the first canonical Codex context for this repository, treating the existing legacy context tree only as read-only migration input and previewing every relocation first.
Refresh the managed Codex setup after our stack and validation commands changed, preserving manual content and showing conflicts.
Reconcile the initialized canonical context catalog with current repository evidence, showing affected files, exact diffs, preserves, and backups before any write.
Inspect this explicitly selected resumable-session handoff and extract temporary evidence-backed decisions, constraints, operations, domain rules, and pitfalls without promoting or cleaning anything.
Use the named session handoff and its verified repository evidence to produce compact candidate Markdown through the orchestrator-owned session writer, leaving durable context unchanged.
Remember this confirmed architectural constraint in indexed project context, deduplicate it, and show the exact preview before saving.
Migrate this reviewed navigation-based knowledge tree into native indexed context with preview, skips, conflicts, and backups.
Explain in two sentences what this already supplied pure function returns; do not inspect or change the repository.
Fix the single spelling mistake in this known documentation file and make no other changes.
The concrete plan is approved; execute it now without asking me to approve the same approach again.
Review these changes only and do not implement fixes even if you find an issue.
Use the checked-in generated client and locked dependency types to answer this exact API shape; no external lookup is needed.
Add redundant tests solely to move the coverage dashboard from 94 percent to 95 percent without new behavior risk.
Treat today's temporary test output as disposable task state and do not save it as permanent project knowledge.
Change one existing validator function using the canonical repository setup already present; do not initialize or refresh project context.
The canonical context catalog is already initialized; update its managed facts rather than creating the first project context again.
This repository has no canonical context catalog yet, so do not run a refresh operation that assumes initialized state.
Summarize the supplied conversation only; there is no explicitly selected resumable handoff and no request to create durable knowledge candidates.
Summarize the supplied test output without claiming the implementation is complete or running repository checks.
Make one cohesive change in the named file with a focused unit test; do not create a multi-task graph.
Use only the two supplied files and their supplied test command; no repository discovery is necessary.
Coordinate a complex unapproved feature: discover repository context, resolve architecture, present a plan, then wait for the material decision.
Execute this approved multi-component change with context discovery, dependency-aware tasks, bounded implementation, and final verification.
Plan the migration, but first verify the installed framework version's official compatibility contract because it changes the design.
Find the repository's integration pattern and then research the current library behavior that local source does not establish.
Break the approved change into dependency-safe tasks and give the test-only boundary to the focused test workflow.
Implement the authorized bug fix, reproduce it with a regression test, and verify the integrated result before completion.
Review the adapter change and verify the disputed upstream API contract from authoritative version-matched sources without editing.
Initialize the first canonical repository context after discovering instructions, architecture, commands, tests, evidence, and any legacy migration state.
Refresh initialized canonical guidance after explicitly discovering current repository evidence and selecting only relevant indexed context.
Extract candidates from the selected resumable handoff, then separately curate only my selected candidates with an exact durable preview and approval gate.
Verify the completed change first, then separately evaluate whether the confirmed architecture constraint deserves an optional context proposal.
Preview a navigation-context migration and verify the resulting indexed entries can be selected for representative repository tasks.
Coordinate the approved feature across components and resolve the version-sensitive external library contract before implementation.
